content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 22, 2003

David Bauer
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 1560
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-03-037

Dear Mr. Bauer:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Assemblyman Ken Maddox regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

May Assemblyman Maddox amend the Form 460 filed for his state Senate campaign to reflect that $45,000 of the $110,000 transferred from his committee for his 2002 election to the Assembly is unattributed, this being the money remaining from the committee bank account on January 1, 2001?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Pursuant to regulation 18530.2, because the funds in the campaign bank account of Maddox for Assembly never fell below $45,000 between January 1, 2001, and the date of the transfer of the funds to the Friends of Ken Maddox campaign bank account, Assemblyman Maddox may amend the Form 460 filed for the state Senate campaign to reflect that $45,000 of the funds transferred from Maddox for Assembly to Friends of Ken Maddox is unattributed.

FACTS

Assemblyman Ken Maddox won election to the Assembly in 2000 with his committee, Maddox for Assembly. In December 2000, that committee was redesignated²

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Note that redesignation of the controlled committee of a candidate for elective state office is no longer permitted pursuant to regulation 18521, which was amended effective May 26, 2002.

File No. A-03-037
Page No. 2

for the 2002 election to the Assembly, which Assemblyman Maddox won. On December
31, 2000, there was approximately $58,000 on deposit in the committee bank account. In
a telephone conversation with Commission staff on April 1, 2003, you confirmed that this
amount remained in the committee bank account on January 1, 2001 (the effective date of
Proposition 34). In December 2002, Assemblyman Maddox opened Friends of Ken
Maddox with the intent of running for state Senate. At that time, $110,000 was
transferred from Maddox for Assembly to Friends of Ken Maddox. On the Form 460,
filed by Friends of Ken Maddox for the period during which the transfer occurred, the
entire transfer was attributed to various donors, using the last in first out method. From
December 31, 2000, through the time of the transfer, the balance in the bank account of
Maddox for Assembly did not drop below $45,000.

ANALYSIS

On January 17, 2003, the Commission adopted regulation 18530.2 as an
emergency regulation. The regulation became operative on January 30, 2003. The
Commission permanently adopted the regulation on May 9, 2003.

Regulation 18530.2(a) provides:

"(a) Candidates for Elective State Office Other Than
Statewide Elective Office:
Pursuant to Government Code section 85306, subdivision
(b), a candidate for elective state office, other than a
candidate for statewide elective office, may transfer funds
possessed on January 1, 2001, to a controlled committee of
the same candidate for an election held after January 1,
2001 without attributing the funds to specific contributors.
There is no limit to the number of times the candidate's
committee may transfer without attribution funds possessed
on January 1, 2001; provided, however, that the maximum
amount that the controlled committee holding the funds on
January 1, 2001 may subsequently transfer without
attribution shall be the lesser of (i) the balance on January
1, 2001 less the amount of any transfers made without
attribution after January 1, 2001, and (ii) the lowest balance
in the committee's account on or after January 1, 2001;
and, provided further, that the maximum amount that any
committee that has received such unattributed transfer may
subsequently transfer without attribution shall be the lesser
of (i) the sum received without attribution less any transfers
subsequently made by that committee without attribution,
and (ii) the lowest balance in the committee's account
following receipt of such unattributed transfer."

File No. A-03-037
Page No. 3

Because the balance in the bank account of Maddox for Assembly was never less than $45,000 from January 1, 2001, through the time of the transfer of the funds to Friends of Ken Maddox, pursuant to regulation 18530.2, Assemblyman Maddox may amend the Form 460 his committee filed to reflect that $45,000 of the $110,000 transferred from his committee for his 2002 election to the Assembly is unattributed.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Holly B. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
